g.data-narration: Welcome to Pattern Classification using Prototype Matching with the Minimum Distance Classifier.
Pattern classification assigns labels to images based on features like edges and textures for computer vision.
g.text: Assigning class labels to input images based on extracted features.
Features: Edge maps, texture, shapes, colors

g.data-narration: Prototype matching compares input images with pre-defined templates representing each class.
g.text: Compare input with pre-defined prototypes (templates) that represent each class.
Goal: Find the prototype that best matches the input

g.data-narration: The Minimum Distance Classifier assigns labels based on the closest prototype using distance metrics.
g.text: Assigns class label based on MINIMUM distance between input and prototypes.

g.data-narration: First, preprocess the input image to extract relevant features like edges or textures.
g.text: Extract features from input image before classification.

g.data-narration: Extract prototypes from training data - each prototype represents a characteristic image of a class.
g.text: Extract prototypes from training dataset. Each prototype represents a class.

g.data-narration: Calculate distance between input features and each prototype using Euclidean or Mahalanobis distance.
g.text: Calculate distance between input features and each prototype.

g.data-narration: Euclidean distance is the straight-line distance between two points in n-dimensional space.
g.text: Straight-line distance between two points in n-dimensional space.

g.data-narration: Mahalanobis distance accounts for feature correlations using the covariance matrix for more accurate classification.
g.text: Accounts for correlations between features using covariance matrix.

g.data-narration: The classifier assigns the class label of the prototype with minimum distance to the input pattern.
g.text: Assign class of prototype with minimum distance.

g.data-narration: Example: classifying vehicle images as cars, trucks, or buses based on edge features.
g.text: Three classes: Cars, Trucks, Buses with edge features

g.data-narration: Advantages include simplicity, robustness to noise, and flexibility with different distance metrics.
Disadvantages include sensitivity to distance metric choice and computational complexity for large datasets.
